title: Mailchimp vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and Drip email marketing platforms. Learn pricing, features, and which tool suits your business needs.
h1: Mailchimp vs Drip: Email Marketing Platform Comparison
intro: Mailchimp and Drip are both popular email marketing platforms, but they cater to different business types and budgets. Mailchimp offers a generous free tier ideal for beginners and small businesses, while Drip specializes in e-commerce integration with advanced automation. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right tool for your marketing goals.
choose_a_if: You're a beginner or small business with a limited budget
You want to start with a free plan and upgrade later
You need a drag-and-drop editor with basic automation and landing pages
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and need deep shopping cart integration
You want advanced revenue attribution and behavioral tracking
You need SMS marketing capabilities alongside email campaigns

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Mailchimp is better for beginners and budget-conscious businesses due to its free tier and simplicity. Drip is better for e-commerce businesses that need advanced tracking, revenue attribution, and store integration. While both have strong ratings, Drip edges out with slightly better G2 reviews and significantly higher Trustpilot satisfaction.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or Drip?
faq.a: Mailchimp is significantly cheaper, with a free plan supporting up to 500 subscribers and paid plans starting at $13/month. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. For budget-conscious businesses, Mailchimp is the clear winner on price, though Drip's premium features may justify the cost for e-commerce sellers.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms support contact list exports, allowing you to migrate your subscriber data. Both Mailchimp and Drip provide standard export options, though you may lose some automation history during the transition. It's recommended to back up your data and test the import before fully switching.
